name: Peñón del Pez – running circular from Peñalsordo
name_original: Peñón del Pez – running circular desde Peñalsordo
region_intro: Jogging around Peñalsordo offers diverse terrain and scenic routes within the province of Badajoz. The region features notable natural landmarks such as Cerro Masatrigo, a conical hill rising to about 500 meters, and the expansive La Serena Reservoir. Varied landscapes include areas like Peñón del Pez, known for its rock formations and biodiversity. These features provide a range of running environments, from technical ascents to paths along reservoir shores. Best jogging routes around Peñalsordo The most popular jogging route is Cerro Masatrigo Botanical Trail, a 4.2 miles (6.8 km) difficult trail that takes about 50 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the surrounding area from Cerro Masatrigo. Another top favourite among local runners is Peñón del Pez – running circular from Peñalsordo, a difficult 5.4 miles (8.7 km) path. This trail explores the impressive landscape and rich biodiversity around Peñón del Pez. Local runners also love the Capilla and its Castle loop from Peñalsordo, an 8.5 miles (13.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 1 minute. Jogging around Peñalsordo is defined by conical hills like Cerro Masatrigo, the shores of La Serena Reservoir, and biodiversity-rich areas such as Peñón del Pez. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 10 runners have used komoot to explore Peñalsordo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Peñalsordo?
A: There are 6 dedicated jogging routes around Peñalsordo listed in this guide. These routes offer a variety of experiences, from challenging ascents to scenic paths along the reservoir.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners in Peñalsordo?
A: While many routes in Peñalsordo are rated as 'difficult' due to elevation changes, there is one 'moderate' option: the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31455189" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de Santa Ana loop from Peñalsordo&lt;/a&gt;. This route is 8.3 miles (13.4 km) long and offers a less strenuous experience compared to others.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see while jogging in Peñalsordo?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Masatrigo&lt;/a&gt;, known as 'the magic mountain' for its conical shape, and enjoy views of the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Serena Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3687215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Peñón del Pez&lt;/a&gt; also offers impressive landscapes and rich biodiversity.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Peñalsordo area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991754988/penon-del-pez-running-circular-from-penalsordo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón del Pez – running circular from Peñalsordo&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31455202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capilla and its Castle loop from Peñalsordo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Which running routes offer the best views in Peñalsordo?
A: For breathtaking panoramic views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992985001/cerro-masatrigo-botanical-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Masatrigo Botanical Trail&lt;/a&gt;. The ascent to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Masatrigo&lt;/a&gt; provides wonderful 360-degree vistas of the surrounding area and the La Serena Reservoir. Another excellent option for views is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38147387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Peñón del Pez – Capilla and its Castle loop from Peñalsordo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails around Peñalsordo?
A: Generally, the outdoor trails around Peñalsordo are dog-friendly, making it a great region to enjoy with your canine companion. However, always ensure your dog is under control, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and check for any specific local regulations on individual routes.

Q: Are there any historical sites or castles to see along the jogging routes?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31455202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capilla and its Castle loop from Peñalsordo&lt;/a&gt; will take you past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3687196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Capilla&lt;/a&gt;, offering a blend of natural beauty and cultural interest during your run.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for running in Peñalsordo?
A: The terrain around Peñalsordo is diverse, ranging from technical sectors with loose rocks, especially on ascents like Cerro Masatrigo, to more accessible paths along the shores of the La Serena Reservoir. You can expect varied landscapes, including hills, rock formations, and areas rich in biodiversity.

Q: What makes Peñalsordo a unique destination for runners?
A: Peñalsordo stands out for its dramatic natural landscapes, such as the perfectly conical &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Masatrigo&lt;/a&gt; and the impressive rock formations of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3687215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón del Pez&lt;/a&gt;. The combination of challenging ascents, scenic reservoir views, and rich biodiversity provides a unique and rewarding running experience.

Q: How long do the running routes in Peñalsordo typically take to complete?
A: The duration varies depending on the route and your pace. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992985001/cerro-masatrigo-botanical-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Masatrigo Botanical Trail&lt;/a&gt;, at 4.2 miles (6.8 km), typically takes about 50 minutes. Longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31455202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capilla and its Castle loop from Peñalsordo&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.5 miles (13.8 km), can take around 2 hours.

Q: Are there any routes that combine views with a visit to a specific landmark?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38147387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Peñón del Pez – Capilla and its Castle loop from Peñalsordo&lt;/a&gt; offers both stunning views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3687215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón del Pez&lt;/a&gt; and passes by the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3687196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Capilla&lt;/a&gt;, providing a comprehensive experience.
tips: The climb to the top of the Rock is hard, long straights, and asphalt in poor condition, although on the other hand it offers good views.
